description: Multi-dimensional competitive scoring matrix. Scores are 1-10. DanteHarvest scores are current build state. Competitor scores are estimated from public docs/repos/demos.

dimensions.label: Evidence Chain Robustness
dimensions.description: Append-only JSONL chain with SHA-256 per entry, sequence numbers, fsync, atomic write, integrity verification. Captures every state change from ingest through promotion.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Add Merkle-root manifest sealing so any truncation of the chain is cryptographically detectable

dimensions.label: Rights Model Completeness
dimensions.description: 7 SourceClass values, 6 TrainingEligibility values, RetentionClass, RightsProfile attached at ingest. Unique depth â€” no competitor models training rights at artifact level. Gap: no GDPR auto-expiry enforcement, no real-world production validation yet.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Already best-in-class. Add GDPR retention enforcement (auto-expiry on RetentionClass.TRANSIENT).

dimensions.label: Pack Promotion Pipeline
dimensions.description: 6-gate promotion: provenance, rights, replay_pass_rate, deterministic_graph, redaction_complete, human_signoff. Sealed EvidenceReceipt with SHA-256. No competitor has anything analogous.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Add automated re-promotion on rights status change (currently requires manual trigger)

dimensions.label: Vector Search Integration
dimensions.description: EmbeddingEngine (384-dim) wired as default when sentence-transformers available; TF-IDF fallback; Qdrant upgrade path. Gap: dense index is an O(n) cosine scan with no persistence, no HNSW/ANN, no hybrid search, no reranking. sentence-transformers is an optional dep. LlamaIndex has 150+ backends, HNSW, sparse+dense hybrid, cross-encoder reranking â€” genuinely superior.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Add local sentence-transformers embedding as default when model is cached; fall back to TF-IDF on cold start

dimensions.label: Replay Harness Fidelity
dimensions.description: Step-by-step executor with pass/fail, chain entry per step, pass_rate aggregation, custom executor injection. Gap: default executor is noop â€” replay validates schema, not actual execution. OpenAdapt (7) actually replays UI actions in a real browser with screenshot comparison. We're tied on infrastructure, behind on actual execution fidelity.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Add Playwright-based step executor so replay runs in a real browser sandbox, not just a noop executor

dimensions.label: Redaction Accuracy
dimensions.description: 14 regex patterns (secrets + PII). NERRedactor written but spaCy fails to load on dev machine (numpy/h5py binary incompatibility) â€” NER is code, not a running capability. Haystack uses Presidio (Microsoft, production-grade, 50+ recognizers, multi-language). Our working capability is regex-only; NER is aspirational until deps are fixed.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Add presidio (MIT) or spaCy NER for context-aware PII detection beyond regex patterns

dimensions.label: CLI Completeness
dimensions.description: 12 commands: ingest file/url/batch, crawl (--sitemap), run create/status, observe browser, watch <dir>, pack list/promote/export, stats, serve, version. Gap: no TUI/progress bars, no streaming output, no shell autocomplete, no config file, no --format flag. Aider (9) has interactive TUI, streaming diffs, /model, /voice, git integration. We're solid but not polished.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Add `harvest ingest batch <dir>` for directory sweep + `harvest watch <dir>` for filesystem monitoring

dimensions.label: Observation Plane Depth
dimensions.description: Browser session + desktop keyboard/mouse events + video keyframe extraction (cv2, optional dep). Gap: no real-time streaming, no 24/7 continuous mode, no per-frame OCR pipeline by default, cv2 is optional. Screenpipe (9) does 24/7 continuous capture with OCR on every frame, real-time audio transcription, app-level activity tracking, semantic search over recorded history â€” fundamentally a different category of capability.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Wire keyframe extractor (harvest_normalize/ocr/keyframes.py) into session_recorder so video files are auto-decomposed

dimensions.label: Taxonomy Builder
dimensions.description: Co-occurrence frequency analysis over WorkflowPack steps. Produces TaxonomyGraph with nodes + edges. Local-first (no LLM required). Gap: simple bigram co-occurrence is basic text mining. LlamaIndex KnowledgeGraphIndex uses LLM to extract subject-predicate-object triples, supports Neo4j, full graph visualization. Comparable depth, different approach.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Add LLM-enhanced taxonomy enrichment path (optional, local-first: uses Ollama or OpenAI when configured)

dimensions.label: Crawl Acquisition
dimensions.description: Crawl4AI adapter (HTTP + optional Playwright) + Crawlee adapter (RequestQueue + link following). Gap: no JS-heavy site rendering by default, no sitemap.xml parsing, no robots.txt-aware depth limiting.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Add sitemap.xml parser to seed RequestQueue; add robots.txt crawl-delay respect to Crawlee adapter

dimensions.label: Transcription Quality
dimensions.description: WhisperAdapter (local + OpenAI API, word timestamps, to_segments). SpeakerDiarizer code written but pyannote.audio requires a HuggingFace auth token and model download â€” not zero-config. Gap: batch-only, no real-time streaming, diarizer setup friction. Screenpipe (9) runs Whisper continuously in real-time with speaker labeling in production. We're batch-first with a complex diarization setup path.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Add pyannote.audio (MIT) for speaker diarization so multi-speaker sessions produce per-speaker transcript segments

dimensions.label: Constitutional Doctrine Enforcement
dimensions.description: robots.txt hard stop (fail-closed on network error), AGPL quarantine, one-door doctrine, append-only chain, local-first default, zero-ambiguity APIs. Unique in the space.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Already best-in-class. Add constitutional test suite that verifies each guarantee via adversarial testing.

best_competitor.name: None â€” no competitor tracks this

dimensions.label: Test Coverage
dimensions.description: 299 unit tests covering all planes; heavily mocked. Gap: no property-based tests (Hypothesis), no integration tests against real external services, no E2E pipeline tests, no CI coverage badge. LlamaIndex has thousands of tests including real integration suites for every provider â€” claiming we beat them at 9 vs 8 is not credible.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Add property-based tests with Hypothesis for chain integrity and receipt tamper detection

dimensions.label: DanteAgents Integration
dimensions.description: HarvestHandoff schema, DanteAgentsExporter, consumption_hints per pack type, typed export bundle. Only DanteHarvest produces this format.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Add streaming export (emit packs as they're promoted, not batch-only) for real-time DanteAgents consumption

dimensions.label: Local-First Guarantee
dimensions.description: Zero network calls by default across all planes. All storage on local filesystem. Remote sync requires explicit RunContract field. Unique depth of commitment.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Already best-in-class. Add encrypted-at-rest option for local storage (AES-256 via cryptography package).

best_competitor.name: Screenpipe (local-first, but cloud sync default in UI)

dimensions.label: AGPL Quarantine
dimensions.description: AGPL dependencies (Firecrawl, Skyvern, OpenRecall) never imported in harvest_* packages. Called as external services only. No other tool tracks this.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Already best-in-class. Add CI gate (import-linter or license-checker) to enforce AGPL quarantine automatically.

best_competitor.name: None â€” no competitor tracks this

dimensions.label: Multi-Format Ingest
dimensions.description: ~12 formats: PDF, DOCX, PPTX, images, video, audio, XLSX/CSV, EPUB, HTML, TXT, MD. DocumentNormalizer dispatcher. Gap: LlamaIndex has 150+ loaders â€” Notion, Slack, GitHub, Discord, SQL databases, Google Drive, email, Twitter, MongoDB, S3, YouTube, Wikipedia. We handle core document formats; they handle every data source. Not comparable at 9 vs 9.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Add spreadsheet ingest (openpyxl: MIT) to FileIngestor for XLSX/CSV with table-to-markdown conversion

best_competitor.name: LlamaIndex (50+ loaders: Excel, Notion, Slack, email, etc.)

dimensions.label: UI / Reviewer Workflow
dimensions.description: FastAPI JSON API: list/detail/approve/reject/chain endpoints. No web UI â€” reviewing packs requires curl or writing a client. Paperless-ngx (8) has a full browser UI with document viewer, tagging, workflows. Rewind.ai (9) has a polished Mac app with timeline, fuzzy search, clip sharing. Our API is a foundation for a UI, not the UI itself.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Build harvest_ui/reviewer/server.py â€” a FastAPI server serving a React reviewer UI: pack diff, approve/reject, merge/split segments, confidence band visualization

best_competitor.name: Rewind.ai (polished Mac UI with timeline, search, clip sharing)

dimensions.label: Specialization Pack Quality
dimensions.description: SpecializationPack with domain, knowledge_refs, workflow_refs, skill_refs, glossary, taxonomy, disallowed_actions. Unique typed schema. Gap: no versioning/semver diff, no migration between versions, no CLI tooling to build specializations from templates, unproven at scale in real deployments.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Add pack versioning with semver diff (breaking vs additive changes) and migration path between pack versions

dimensions.label: Eval Harness
dimensions.description: EvalPack, ReplayHarness, 6-gate promotion, pass_rate, chain entry per step, cases_from_workflow. Gap: default executor is noop â€” eval validates pack schema, not actual step execution. No LLM-as-judge, no RAGAS metrics, no semantic similarity scoring. Haystack (7) runs live LLM evaluation with faithfulness, answer relevancy, context recall metrics.
dimensions.sprint_goal: Add LLM-as-judge eval mode: optional oracle that uses Claude/GPT to score step outputs against oracle_rules

sprint_queue.sprint_goal: Build React SPA reviewer: pack timeline, visual pack diff, approve/reject buttons, confidence band display, segment merge/split. This is the single highest-leverage sprint.
sprint_queue.rationale: API exists but no web UI means operators can't review packs without writing curl commands. A 4â†’8 move would add +36 weighted points.

sprint_queue.sprint_goal: Add Playwright-based JS rendering as default (not optional) in CrawleeAdapter; LLM content extraction schema; async parallel crawl with configurable concurrency
sprint_queue.rationale: Firecrawl gap is real â€” JS-heavy sites produce no content with our HTTP-only adapter.

sprint_queue.sprint_goal: Add Playwright step executor so replay actually navigates a real browser and captures screenshots for diff validation
sprint_queue.rationale: noop executor makes eval tests meaningless as validation. OpenAdapt does real browser replay at 7 â€” we need to overtake.

sprint_queue.sprint_goal: Add continuous background capture mode with configurable interval; wire OCR into keyframe pipeline by default (not optional); add semantic search over captured frames
Replace spaCy NER with Microsoft Presidio (MIT license) which ships pre-built recognizers and works without numpy/h5py; add multi-language support
sprint_queue.rationale: spaCy broken on dev machine due to numpy ABI mismatch. Presidio is a drop-in with better defaults.

sprint_queue.sprint_goal: Swap O(n) cosine scan for usearch or hnswlib (MIT) for ANN search; add persistence for the dense index; add hybrid TF-IDF + dense scoring
Add LLM-as-judge eval mode with oracle_rules; add RAGAS-style faithfulness + relevancy metrics; add visual diff comparison for screenshot-based replay

meta.scoring_note: Scores reflect working, running capabilities â€” not aspirational code. Broken deps (spaCy numpy ABI), optional deps (cv2, sentence-transformers), and API-only features (no web UI) are scored at capability level, not implementation level.
